what_exists_and_flaws: Dozens of nearly identical landlord income/expense spreadsheets on Etsy priced $3-$37. They record data but produce zero actionable insight. A landlord with 6 properties sees totals, not decisions. No tool tells them which property is underperforming net of maintenance costs, which tenant is a late-payment risk pattern, or when a unit's cap rate has degraded below refinance threshold. They are ledgers, not advisors.
what_to_build: A multi-property Decision Support Spreadsheet (Google Sheets) that actively generates plain-English verdicts: 'Property 3 on Elm St has the lowest net yield at 4.1% — maintenance costs have grown 34% YoY and are eating your rent increase. Consider a rent adjustment or deferred maintenance audit before renewal.' Includes a per-property profit ranking, late-payment frequency scoring per tenant, and a cash-flow trend alert system. Not a tracker — a monthly briefing generator.
ai_explanation_value: The AI explanation layer converts ratio outputs into prioritized landlord actions: instead of showing NOI = $740, it says 'This is your worst-performing unit. If you raised rent $150/mo to match comps, your portfolio yield improves from 5.2% to 6.8% — that is the single highest-ROI action available to you this quarter.' Transforms passive data into a ranked to-do list.
suggested_etsy_title: Rental Property Decision Dashboard | AI-Explained Portfolio Analysis | Which Property Makes You Money | Landlord Google Sheets
target_customer: Self-managing landlord with 3-10 rental units who does their own bookkeeping and feels overwhelmed comparing property performance

what_exists_and_flaws: Property maintenance trackers on Etsy ($1-$24) are glorified repair log forms — date, description, cost, vendor. The best ones add a scheduling tab. None of them connect repair history to financial consequences, flag when a system (HVAC, roof, plumbing) is approaching replacement economics, or help owners decide whether to repair vs. replace vs. raise rent to cover capital costs. The 'Property Maintenance Software OS' at $23.80 is the closest but reviewers note it is mostly a checklist system.
what_to_build: A Repair-to-ROI Decision Engine for property managers and landlords. Tracks repair history by system and unit, then generates verdicts: 'The HVAC in Unit 4 has cost $2,340 in repairs over 18 months. Replacement cost is $4,800. You have crossed the repair-replace threshold — continued repair is now more expensive than financing a new unit over 36 months.' Includes a capital reserve calculator and a maintenance-cost-per-unit profitability impact report.
ai_explanation_value: AI layer answers the question owners actually ask: 'Should I fix it again or replace it?' It calculates annualized repair burn rate per system, projects forward 12 months, and gives a clear recommendation with the dollar difference between repair and replace paths. No owner can do this math in their head — the tool does it automatically on each log entry.
suggested_etsy_title: Property Repair vs Replace Decision Tool | Maintenance Cost ROI Tracker | Landlord Capital Planning Google Sheets Spreadsheet
target_customer: Landlord or small property management company managing 5-25 units who self-manages maintenance and struggles to justify capital expenditure decisions

what_exists_and_flaws: Tenant move-in/move-out checklists on Etsy ($1.69-$37) are purely administrative forms — room-by-room condition fields, signature lines, deposit notes. They help document but make no decisions. No product connects tenant screening data to lease risk scoring, tracks late payment patterns to predict future delinquency, or helps landlords decide whether to renew a lease, raise rent, or begin a turnover process. The highest-priced inspection report at $37 (shop 61829217, rated 3/5) received poor reviews likely because it over-promised on functionality.
what_to_build: A Tenant Profitability and Retention Decision System. Tracks rent payment history, late frequency, maintenance call volume per tenant, and lease-end timing. Outputs: 'Tenant in Unit 2B has paid late 4 of 12 months, submitted 7 maintenance requests vs. portfolio average of 2.1, and costs you 31% more to service than your average tenant. Renewal recommendation: Do not renew at current rate — raise by $200 or begin turnover planning 90 days out.' Includes a tenant ranking and a deposit adequacy flag.
ai_explanation_value: AI layer converts tenant history into a retention vs. turnover ROI calculation: it estimates the cost of turnover (vacancy days x daily rent + cleaning + repairs + re-leasing) versus the cost of keeping a high-maintenance tenant, then recommends the financially superior path. This is a decision no form can make — it requires cross-referencing four data streams simultaneously.
suggested_etsy_title: Tenant Retention Decision Tool | Lease Renewal vs Turnover ROI | Late Payment Risk Scorer | Landlord Google Sheets Dashboard
target_customer: Landlord with 4-15 units who self-manages leases and struggles to make objective renewal decisions without emotional bias

what_exists_and_flaws: Short-term rental (Airbnb/VRBO) spreadsheets on Etsy ($2-$23) track bookings and income by month. They are income statement tools, not optimization tools. None of them compare platform performance (Airbnb vs. VRBO vs. direct), identify which pricing tiers generate the best net margin after cleaning and platform fees, flag occupancy gaps that respond to dynamic pricing, or tell a host which months they should block for personal use vs. rent out for maximum annual yield.
what_to_build: An STR Revenue Optimization Decision System. Hosts log bookings by platform, length of stay, booking lead time, cleaning cost, and platform fee. The tool generates: 'Your 2-night bookings generate $180 net after fees and cleaning vs. $310 for 4-night stays. Blocking minimum stays below 3 nights in July would increase net revenue by an estimated $840.' Includes a platform fee comparison, seasonal rate recommendation engine, and a personal-use opportunity cost calculator.
ai_explanation_value: AI layer identifies counterintuitive patterns owners miss: a host sees high occupancy but low net yield because short stays are filling the calendar. The tool explains why high occupancy is not the goal — net revenue per available night is — and prescribes specific minimum stay and rate floor changes. It replaces expensive STR revenue management consultants for small hosts.
suggested_etsy_title: Airbnb STR Revenue Decision Tool | Platform Profit Comparison | Pricing Strategy Optimizer | Vacation Rental Google Sheets Dashboard
target_customer: Short-term rental host with 1-4 Airbnb or VRBO properties who self-manages and wants to maximize net income without hiring a revenue manager

what_exists_and_flaws: The Etsy market has zero products aimed at small service businesses (HVAC, landscaping, pool service, cleaning, plumbing, pest control) doing crew or truck performance analysis. The closest products are generic bookkeeping spreadsheets. Service businesses with 2-8 crews desperately need to know which crew generates the most profit per hour, which truck is costing more to operate than it earns, and which service routes are worth keeping vs. cutting — but no Etsy product addresses this at all. This is a completely uncontested niche.
what_to_build: A Crew and Truck Profitability Decision Engine for small field service businesses. Owner inputs job revenue, labor hours, drive time, materials, and truck operating costs per crew per week. The system outputs: 'Crew 2 generated $2,340 gross profit this week vs. Crew 1 at $1,610 — a 45% gap driven by 2.1 fewer drive hours per job and 18% lower materials waste. Crew 1 needs route optimization or personnel review.' Includes a truck replacement threshold calculator: 'Truck 3 repair costs have reached $8,200 this year against $6,400 annual lease equivalent — replacement is now economically justified.'
ai_explanation_value: AI layer translates field service data into owner-ready verdicts without requiring accounting knowledge. It identifies the root cause of profit gaps (labor efficiency vs. materials vs. drive time vs. job mix) and ranks corrective actions by dollar impact. For truck replacement, it calculates the break-even month and presents a lease vs. buy vs. continue-operating comparison. A 3-truck HVAC owner cannot do this analysis manually — this tool does it in real time after each weekly data entry.
suggested_etsy_title: Crew Profit Tracker for HVAC Landscaping Plumbing | Truck Replacement Calculator | Field Service Decision Dashboard Google Sheets
target_customer: Owner of a small field service business (HVAC, landscaping, pool service, cleaning, plumbing) with 2-8 crews or trucks who wants to know which crews and vehicles make or lose money

what_exists_and_flaws: A proven Etsy comp, 'HVAC Estimating Spreadsheet | Job Cost Calculator for HVAC Projects,' sells at $175 with a 4.5 rating, proving the market will pay premium for job-costing tools in field service. But it only calculates cost and margin for a single job after the fact -- it doesn't help the owner decide whether to take the job at all, doesn't compare jobs against each other, and gives no guidance on what to quote to hit a target margin.
what_to_build: A Job Bid Decision Engine that goes further than the proven $175 comp: owner enters job scope, materials, labor hours, and drive distance BEFORE quoting. The system outputs a recommended quote price to hit a target margin, flags jobs that are likely to be unprofitable before they're accepted ('This job's margin at your quoted price is 8% -- your breakeven threshold is 22%. Raise the quote to $1,840 or decline.'), and ranks past jobs by profit-per-hour so the owner learns which job types to chase vs. avoid. Same proven category, but a pre-decision tool instead of a post-job ledger.
ai_explanation_value: The AI layer is the entire value proposition over the comp: it doesn't just record what a job cost, it tells the owner what to charge before they commit, and pattern-matches against their own job history to flag red flags a busy owner would miss under quoting pressure -- 'Jobs over 50 miles drive distance have averaged 11% lower margin this year; build in a $75 travel surcharge for jobs this far out.'
suggested_etsy_title: HVAC Job Bid Calculator | Quote Pricing Decision Tool | Job Cost Estimator for Plumbing Electrical Contractors Google Sheets
target_customer: HVAC, plumbing, or electrical contractor who quotes jobs directly and wants to stop underbidding unprofitable work

what_exists_and_flaws: Proven comps 'Real Estate Investment Spreadsheet, Deal Analyzer ROI IRR' ($139.30, 5 rating) and 'Real Estate ROI Calculator, Cashflow Analyzer Pro' ($88.40, 5 rating) show buyers will pay $88-$139 for deal-analysis tools -- well above the rental-tracker median of $11. But both are single-deal calculators: punch in numbers, see ROI. Neither helps an investor evaluating multiple competing properties decide which one to actually buy, and neither explains the result in plain language.
what_to_build: A Buy/Pass/Walk-Away Deal Comparison System for active property buyers. Investor logs 2-5 prospective deals side by side (price, rent, expenses, financing terms) and the system ranks them with a verdict: 'Deal B has the highest cap rate (7.2% vs 5.1%) but Deal A has 40% better cash-on-cash return because of seller financing terms -- if your goal is monthly cash flow, choose A. If building long-term equity, choose B.' Includes a walk-away threshold flag for deals that don't clear minimum return requirements.
ai_explanation_value: The proven comps output numbers (cap rate, IRR) and stop there -- the buyer still has to decide what those numbers mean across multiple competing deals. The AI layer does the comparison and gives the actual buy/pass recommendation aligned to the investor's stated goal (cash flow vs. appreciation vs. equity), which is the decision a deal analyzer alone can't make.
suggested_etsy_title: Real Estate Deal Comparison Tool | Buy or Pass Decision Calculator | Multi-Property ROI Cap Rate Analyzer Google Sheets
target_customer: Active real estate investor evaluating multiple potential property purchases who wants a clear buy/pass verdict, not just raw ROI numbers

what_exists_and_flaws: The proven comp 'Real Estate ROI Calculator, Cashflow Analyzer Pro' ($88.40, 5 rating) calculates current cash flow on a property an investor already owns or is buying -- a snapshot. It does not stress-test that cash flow against rate increases, vacancy spikes, or expense inflation, and gives no guidance on when an owner should refinance, sell, or hold through a downturn. Existing landlord portfolio trackers on Etsy also skip this entirely -- they show what happened, not what could happen.
what_to_build: A Cash Flow Stress Test & Refinance Decision Tool for owners of existing rental property. Owner enters current loan terms, rent, and expenses, then the system models scenarios (rate reset, 2-month vacancy, 15% expense increase) and outputs a verdict: 'Under a 1-month vacancy plus a 20% expense spike, this property's cash flow goes negative for 3 months. Your cash reserve covers 1.4 of those months -- build reserves or refinance the variable-rate loan before year-end.' Includes a refinance break-even calculator (months to recoup closing costs) and a hold-vs-sell signal based on equity growth vs. cash flow trend.
ai_explanation_value: The $88 comp tells an owner their cash flow today. This tool tells them what happens to that cash flow under realistic stress scenarios and what to do about it -- refinance, build reserves, or sell -- before the stress event happens, not after. That forward-looking verdict is the entire decision-support layer the comp is missing.
suggested_etsy_title: Rental Property Cash Flow Stress Test | Refinance or Hold Decision Tool | Vacancy Rate Risk Calculator Google Sheets
target_customer: Landlord or investor who already owns rental property and wants to know how much risk they're carrying before rates rise or a vacancy hits

what_exists_and_flaws: Equipment maintenance trackers on Etsy are generic repair logs with no link to financial consequences. The Crew Profit Tracker's truck-replacement logic proves the repair-vs-replace decision sells, but it is built specifically for trucks. Landscaping owners need the same logic for mowers and trimmers, pool service owners for pumps and trucks, pest control for sprayer rigs, roofers for ladders/lifts and trucks -- and no single product generalizes this decision across equipment types small service businesses actually own.
what_to_build: A general-purpose Equipment Replacement Decision Calculator usable for any piece of revenue-generating equipment (mower, pump, sprayer rig, lift, van). Owner logs repair costs, downtime hours, and resale value per asset; the tool ranks all equipment by 'cost per operating hour' and flags which assets have crossed the replace threshold: 'Mower 3 costs $4.10/operating hour in repairs vs. a fleet average of $1.20 -- financing a replacement at $38/week is now cheaper than keeping it running.' Includes a downtime-cost calculator that converts lost revenue from breakdowns into the replacement decision.
ai_explanation_value: Most owners track repairs in a notebook and decide to replace equipment on gut feel when something breaks for the third time. The AI layer quantifies exactly when repair spend plus lost-revenue-from-downtime exceeds the cost of replacing, and ranks every asset the owner has so they know which one to act on first, not just the one that broke most recently.
suggested_etsy_title: Equipment Replacement Decision Calculator | Repair vs Replace Cost Per Hour Tracker for Landscaping Pool Pest Control Roofing Google Sheets
target_customer: Owner of a landscaping, pool service, pest control, or roofing business with multiple pieces of equipment who decides repair-vs-replace on gut feel

what_exists_and_flaws: Recurring-service businesses (lawn care, pool service, cleaning, pest control) live on repeat customers, but no Etsy product helps owners decide which recurring customers are actually profitable once drive time and service time are accounted for. Generic invoice/CRM templates just list customers and amounts billed -- they don't surface that a customer two towns away at a discounted rate is quietly losing money every visit.
what_to_build: A Recurring Customer Profitability Ranker for subscription-style service businesses. Owner logs each recurring customer's billing amount, visit frequency, drive time, and service time; the tool ranks every customer by profit-per-visit and flags drop/renegotiate candidates: 'Customer #14 generates $12 profit per visit after drive time vs. your $45 average -- raise their rate at next renewal or drop the route.' Includes a route-day profitability rollup so owners see which day's full schedule is actually worth running.
ai_explanation_value: Recurring revenue feels safe, which is exactly why owners keep underpriced customers for years without noticing. The AI layer forces the comparison every owner avoids doing manually -- ranking every customer by true profit, not billed amount -- and recommends a specific action (raise rate, drop, keep) instead of just displaying a number.
suggested_etsy_title: Recurring Customer Profitability Tracker | Which Clients Make You Money Decision Tool for Lawn Care Pool Cleaning Pest Control Google Sheets
target_customer: Owner of a subscription-style service business (lawn care, pool service, residential cleaning, pest control) with 20+ recurring customers who has never calculated true per-visit profit

what_exists_and_flaws: Landscaping, pool service, and HVAC businesses are heavily seasonal, but no Etsy product helps owners decide staffing and cash reserve levels going into the slow season. Generic budget spreadsheets show monthly totals; none model the specific risk of running out of cash during an off-season or tell an owner how many seasonal staff they can actually afford to keep on through winter.
what_to_build: A Seasonal Cash Flow and Staffing Decision Tool. Owner enters peak and off-season revenue history, fixed costs, and current staff costs; the tool projects month-by-month cash position through the off-season and outputs a verdict: 'At current burn rate, cash reserves run out in month 4 of the off-season unless 2 seasonal staff are laid off by November 1, or $14,000 in reserve is built up before season-end.' Includes a layoff-timing calculator showing the cash impact of cutting staff at different dates.
ai_explanation_value: Seasonal owners often discover a cash shortfall only when it happens. The AI layer projects forward from current numbers and gives a specific date and dollar figure for action -- not just a chart of declining cash -- which is the difference between a tracker and a tool that prevents a business from running out of money.
suggested_etsy_title: Seasonal Business Cash Flow Planner | Staffing and Layoff Timing Decision Tool for Landscaping Pool Service HVAC Google Sheets
target_customer: Owner of a seasonal service business (landscaping, pool service, HVAC) who has been caught short on cash or staffing during the slow season before

what_exists_and_flaws: Contractor and roofing businesses quote far more jobs than they win, but no Etsy product tracks win rate by job type, lead source, or price point to tell an owner which jobs to chase and which to stop quoting. Generic CRM/lead trackers log status (won/lost) without surfacing the pattern that, for example, insurance-referral roofing jobs close at 3x the rate of cold-lead jobs at the same price.
what_to_build: A Quote-to-Close Decision Tool for contractors and roofers. Owner logs every quote with job type, lead source, quoted price, and outcome; the tool calculates win rate by lead source and job type and recommends where to focus: 'Insurance-referral leads close at 61% vs. 14% for cold leads at the same average price -- shift marketing spend away from cold outreach toward referral partnerships.' Includes a price-sensitivity flag showing whether losses cluster at specific price thresholds, suggesting where quotes are priced too high.
ai_explanation_value: Contractors usually just feel like 'leads aren't converting' without knowing why. The AI layer isolates which lead source and price combination is actually killing the win rate and gives a specific reallocation recommendation, turning a pile of won/lost rows into a marketing and pricing decision.
suggested_etsy_title: Quote to Close Tracker | Win Rate by Lead Source Decision Tool for Roofing Contractors Pest Control Google Sheets
target_customer: Roofing or general contractor who quotes 10+ jobs a month and doesn't know which lead sources or price points are actually winning

what_exists_and_flaws: No Etsy product helps an established service business owner decide when they've actually outgrown their current crew/truck capacity and should expand. Owners typically decide to hire or buy a new truck reactively, when they're already overwhelmed, rather than from a clear capacity signal.
what_to_build: A Capacity and Expansion Decision Tool. Owner logs weekly job volume, jobs turned down or rescheduled due to capacity, and current crew/truck utilization; the tool tracks a rolling 'turned-away revenue' figure and outputs a verdict: 'You have turned away an estimated $3,200/week in jobs for 6 straight weeks -- a second crew paying for itself requires $2,400/week, so expansion is now cash-flow positive from week one.' Includes a breakeven calculator for the cost of a new crew/truck against captured turned-away revenue.
ai_explanation_value: Most owners expand on gut feel or burnout, not data. The AI layer quantifies exactly how much revenue is being left on the table by staying at current capacity and translates that into a clear, dated go/no-go expansion decision with the breakeven math already done.
suggested_etsy_title: Service Business Expansion Calculator | When to Hire a Crew or Buy a Truck Decision Tool Google Sheets
target_customer: Owner of a growing field service business who keeps turning away jobs and isn't sure if hiring or buying another truck would actually pay off

what_exists_and_flaws: Technician scorecard and KPI tracker listings on Etsy run $2-$120 (median ~$10-28), with one outlier at $689 proving the ceiling exists. But every one is a manager's report card -- it grades a tech on jobs/day, callback rate, revenue -- without ever answering the owner's real question: is this individual technician worth what I pay them, and should I keep, coach, raise, or cut them? They display KPIs; they make no employment decision. The existing catalog covers CREW profitability (groups) and job bidding, but nothing isolates the per-employee keep/coach/cut verdict for an individual hourly tech.
what_to_build: A Technician Keep-Coach-Cut Decision System. Owner logs each tech's loaded labor cost (wage + burden), billable hours, callback/rework rate, and revenue generated. The tool computes profit-per-tech net of rework and outputs a verdict: 'Tech Marcus bills 71% of paid hours and generates $34/hr in gross profit -- your top performer; a $3/hr raise still leaves 9:1 ROI. Tech Dale bills 48% with a 19% callback rate costing $4,100 in unbilled return trips -- coach on first-time-fix or his seat loses money. Replacement breakeven is reached in 7 weeks.' Includes a rework-cost calculator and a wage-to-value ratio ranking across the whole team.
ai_explanation_value: The KPI comps stop at numbers a tech 'looks' busy. The AI layer converts callback rate and billable-hour ratio into the dollar decision owners avoid making emotionally: this specific person earns or loses you money, and here is the raise, the coaching target, or the replacement breakeven date. Crew profitability tools rank teams; this ranks individuals and recommends an HR action per person -- a different decision entirely.
suggested_etsy_title: Technician Profitability Scorecard | Keep Coach or Cut Decision Tool | Per-Employee ROI Tracker for HVAC Plumbing Field Service Google Sheets
target_customer: Owner of an HVAC, plumbing, or field service company with 3-15 hourly technicians who suspects some techs lose money but has never calculated per-employee profit net of callbacks

what_exists_and_flaws: Material/inventory and job-cost spreadsheets on Etsy ($2-$47, median ~$13) track what materials a job consumed AFTER the fact, or list parts on hand. None tell a service owner how much money is silently leaking between purchasing and billing -- the gap between materials bought and materials actually billed to customers. The existing catalog's job-bid tool prices a job before quoting and the crew tool tracks materials waste at the crew level, but neither isolates the systemic markup-leakage and shrinkage decision: am I marking up parts enough, and where is unbilled material disappearing?
what_to_build: A Materials Markup & Leakage Decision Tool. Owner logs material purchases and what was actually billed per job or per month. The tool surfaces effective realized markup vs. target and flags leakage sources: 'You bought $18,400 in parts but only billed $21,000 -- a 14% effective markup against your 35% target. The gap is $6,400/yr in money left on the table. 60% of the leak traces to small parts under $25 that get installed but never added to invoices -- add a flat shop-materials fee of $18/job to recover an estimated $5,100/yr.' Includes a per-supplier price-creep alert and a markup-by-job-type breakdown.
ai_explanation_value: Owners assume their markup is whatever they set on paper; the tool reveals the REALIZED markup after shrinkage, forgotten small parts, and supplier price creep -- almost always far lower than intended. The AI layer pinpoints WHERE the leak is (small parts, a specific supplier, a job type) and prescribes the specific fix (flat materials fee, markup change, supplier switch) with the dollar recovery. No existing tracker connects purchasing to billing this way.
suggested_etsy_title: Materials Markup & Profit Leak Calculator | Where Are You Losing Money on Parts Decision Tool for HVAC Plumbing Contractors Google Sheets
target_customer: Service business owner who marks up materials but suspects parts and small items are quietly going unbilled and eroding margin

what_exists_and_flaws: Marketing/lead trackers and 'service business dashboard' listings ($1-$699, median ~$14) log where leads come from and maybe count them. The existing catalog's quote-to-close tool measures WIN RATE by lead source once a lead is in hand. But nothing measures the decision upstream of that: what does each marketing channel actually COST per acquired customer, and what is the lifetime value of customers from that channel, so the owner knows which marketing to scale, keep, or kill. Win rate and channel ROI are different decisions -- a channel can win cheaply but deliver low-value one-time customers.
what_to_build: A Marketing Channel ROI & Cost-Per-Customer Decision Tool. Owner enters monthly spend per channel (Google LSA, Facebook, yard signs, truck wraps, Angi, door hangers, referral bonuses) and customers/revenue attributed to each. The tool computes true cost-per-acquired-customer and channel-level ROI including repeat revenue: 'Google LSA costs $84 per customer who averages $610 lifetime value -- 7.3x ROI, scale this. Angi costs $61 per customer but those convert to one-time jobs averaging $190 -- 3.1x and declining; cap spend. Yard signs cost $9 per customer at $540 LTV -- your hidden best channel; triple the order.' Includes a wasted-spend flag and a reallocation recommendation.
ai_explanation_value: The win-rate tool tells owners which leads close; this tells them which DOLLARS spent come back as profit, factoring repeat value the owner never attributes back to the original channel. The AI layer ranks every channel by real ROI and hands the owner a scale/keep/kill budget reallocation -- a spending decision, not a conversion decision. Distinct from quote-to-close, which assumes the lead already exists.
suggested_etsy_title: Marketing ROI & Cost Per Customer Calculator | Which Ads Actually Pay Off Decision Tool for HVAC Landscaping Cleaning Pest Control Google Sheets
target_customer: Service business owner spending on multiple lead channels (Google, Angi, Facebook, signs) who doesn't know which marketing actually returns a profit

what_exists_and_flaws: Route and 'route profitability tracker' listings ($1-$64, median ~$10) and the existing catalog's recurring-customer profitability ranker both evaluate individual customers/stops. But no product helps the owner make the DENSITY decision -- whether the geographic shape of a route or service area is itself the problem, and which new customers to accept or decline based on where they sit. The recurring-customer tool says 'drop customer #14'; this answers a different question: 'should I take on this new prospect 18 miles out, and is my whole west-side territory worth servicing at all?'
what_to_build: A Service Area & Route Density Decision Tool. Owner maps customers into zones (zip clusters) with revenue and drive time per zone. The tool computes revenue-per-drive-hour and density per zone and outputs accept/decline rules: 'Your north zone yields $140/drive-hour with 12 stops in 6 miles -- accept any new customer here, even at a discount. Your far-west zone yields $38/drive-hour with 3 scattered stops -- decline new west-side prospects unless they pay a $40 distance premium, and consider selling that route to a competitor.' Includes a new-customer accept/decline calculator that scores a prospect by their zone before you book them, and a minimum-price-by-distance table.
ai_explanation_value: Per-customer profitability tools react to customers you already have; this is a forward-looking gatekeeping and territory decision. The AI layer turns drive geography into a rule the owner applies at booking time -- accept, decline, or surcharge based on zone density -- and flags whole territories worth abandoning. Owners grow blindly into unprofitable sprawl; this makes the 'where should I and shouldn't I serve' decision explicit.
suggested_etsy_title: Service Area Density & Route Profit Calculator | Accept or Decline New Customers by Zone Decision Tool for Lawn Care Pool Pest Control Cleaning Google Sheets
target_customer: Owner of a route-based service business (lawn care, pool, pest control, cleaning) deciding whether to accept far-flung new customers and whether outlying territory is worth keeping

what_exists_and_flaws: Pricing and 'service company profit tracker' spreadsheets ($2-$70, median ~$11) record what owners charged. None help a service owner decide whether and how much to RAISE prices across their existing book without losing the customers who actually matter. Service owners chronically undercharge and freeze on raising rates because they fear churn. The existing catalog covers per-customer profitability and bidding NEW jobs, but nothing models the portfolio-wide rate-increase decision: what happens to total profit if I raise everyone 8%, and which customers can I afford to lose?
what_to_build: A Price Increase Decision Simulator. Owner enters current customer rates, costs, and a churn-risk guess per segment; the tool models the profit outcome of different across-the-board or tiered increases: 'A flat 8% increase grows annual profit $11,200 even if you lose your 6 most price-sensitive customers -- because those 6 generate only $9/visit in profit anyway. Raising premium-tier customers 5% and discount-tier 12% maximizes profit at $14,600. Your break-even churn is 23%: you can lose nearly a quarter of customers and still come out ahead.' Includes a per-customer 'safe to raise' flag and a script-timing recommendation.
ai_explanation_value: The single decision service owners most avoid is raising prices, because they can't see the math past the fear of losing customers. The AI layer computes the break-even churn rate -- the percentage of customers you could lose and STILL make more money -- and shows that the customers most likely to leave are the least profitable ones. It converts a paralyzing emotional decision into a quantified, almost always favorable one. No tracker simulates the churn-vs-profit tradeoff.
suggested_etsy_title: Price Increase Decision Calculator | How Much Can I Raise Rates Without Losing Money | Churn Breakeven Tool for Cleaning Lawn Pool HVAC Service Google Sheets
target_customer: Service business owner who knows they undercharge but is afraid to raise prices and wants to know exactly how much churn they can survive

what_exists_and_flaws: Service businesses bleed profit on jobs they have to redo for free -- warranty callbacks, comebacks, re-cleans -- but no Etsy product treats this as a measurable, decidable problem. Work-order and technician trackers ($1-$120) log jobs but never aggregate the cost of work done twice. The existing catalog's technician scorecard (proposed above) flags rework per individual; this is the orthogonal owner-level decision: what is rework costing the whole business, which job types and root causes drive it, and is it cheaper to fix the process or accept the loss?
what_to_build: A Callback & Rework Cost Decision Tool. Owner logs every comeback/redo with job type, cause (wrong diagnosis, install error, missing part, customer expectation), and the unbilled hours+materials it consumed. The tool totals the annual cost of rework and ranks root causes: 'Callbacks cost you $19,800/yr -- 7% of revenue done for free. 44% trace to one cause: incomplete first-visit diagnosis on AC jobs. A $1,200 diagnostic-tool purchase plus a 2-line checklist would recover an estimated $8,700/yr. Comebacks on drain jobs are within tolerance -- leave them alone.' Includes a cost-of-quality dashboard and a fix-the-process vs. live-with-it ROI calculator per cause.
ai_explanation_value: Rework is invisible because it never appears on an invoice -- owners feel busy without knowing 7% of revenue is being given away. The AI layer makes the cost concrete, isolates the few root causes responsible for most of it (Pareto), and tells the owner which fixes pay for themselves and which problems are cheaper to tolerate. That fix-vs-tolerate verdict per cause is a process-investment decision no existing product makes.
suggested_etsy_title: Callback & Rework Cost Calculator | What Are Free Redo Jobs Really Costing You Decision Tool for HVAC Plumbing Cleaning Roofing Google Sheets
target_customer: Service business owner whose crews redo jobs for free and who has never measured what callbacks and comebacks cost or which problems are worth fixing
